California
Fair Political
Practices Comrnission
December 19, 1990
Douqlas Chandler
1355 42nd Street
Sacramento, CA 9 5819
Re: Your Request for written Advice
our File No. r -9 0- 711
Dear Mr. Chandler:
This is in response to your request for written advice
concerning the revolving door provisions of the Political 
Reform
Act, (the t'Act" ) . r
Your request for advice has been denied because your query
concerns past conduct on your part. 
Regulat.ion l-8329 (copy
enclosed) delineates in detail requirements for the rendering of
advice, formal, and informal, from the commission. Subdivision
(b) (8) (A) of that regulation states that the Commission may refuse
to provide formal written advice whj-ch relates to past conduct. In
the same vein, subdivision (c) (4) (A) of the same regulation states
that the Commission may refuse to furnish inforrnal advice if the
advice sought relates to past conduct.
Your Letter also refers to several' telephone conversations
between you and )-ega] division staff nembers. Although the I'etter
does not nane the staff mernbers or state the dates of the
conversations, you state that you !/ere ttguidedtr in your consulting
activities by advice aIlegedly furnished by the staff members'
Please be advised that our records of conversations betv/een you
and staff members are not consistent with your assertions of
advice given You bY teJ.ePhone.
You stated to rne in our telephone conversation of
December 10, 1990, that you are no longer making your livelihood
fron consul-ting in the field of bond issuance. since your letter
seeks advice r6garding the application of the revolving door
statutes to bon6 issuince pro-eedings and you are no }onger in
that field, 
$re cannot respond to your request for advi'ce' If

File No. r-9 0-711

there are facts extant that tend to sho$/ that you are requesting
advice for prospective behavior rather that past conduct, please
feel, free to contact us to di-scuss the natter.
Additionally, if you should re-enter the bond issuance

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions restrict consulting activities related to bond issuance proceedings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide formal or informal written advice because the query relates to past conduct. The requester is no longer engaged in bond issuance consulting, making prospective guidance inapplicable, though the FPPC indicated willingness to assist if the requester re-enters that field with prospective questions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter rather than a standard advice letter. No formal Question or Conclusion sections exist; advice was denied under Regulation 1-8329 because it addressed past conduct. Document includes discussion of inconsistencies between requester's claims about prior telephone advice and FPPC records.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions restrict consulting activities related to bond issuance proceedings?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide formal or informal written advice because the query relates to past conduct. The requester is no longer engaged in bond issuance consulting, making prospective guidance inapplicable, though the FPPC indicated willingness to assist if the requester re-enters that field with prospective questions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC denied Douglas Chandler's request for written advice regarding revolving door provisions of the Political Reform Act, citing that his query concerns past conduct and that he is no longer working in bond issuance consulting.
